$comment: Runnable eval definitions for /commit (spec docs/spec-portable-ai-procedures.md §4.1). Converted from the original repo-root evals/evals.json per the approved conversion manifest (docs/spec-skill-evals-manifest.md — Phase 1 of docs/spec-skill-evals-baseline.md). EXECUTION NOTICE: skill-eval prompts are never executed against the real repo or real GitHub — any skill, any origin. `kind: execution` entries require a harness-built sandbox (`fixture` names the starting-state profile; `human_script` gives the scripted human replies, valid only inside a marker-bearing sandbox) — see docs/strategy-skill-evals.md. `kind: routing` entries test whether/how the skill fires in a live session; a subagent handed this file cannot execute them — they stay documented manual runbooks (docs/strategy-skill-evals.md) until the Phase-8 session runner. As of Phase 1 the sandbox harness does not exist yet (it ships in Phase 2), so no eval below is executable at all right now — CLAUDE.md's guardrail carries this staged caveat too.

evals.preconditions: On a dirty feature branch (not main); a handful of modified files in src/ related to the profile-redirect bug; no .env or generated artifacts in the diff; gh is authenticated.
evals.human_script: At the step-14 bundled approval checkpoint, the human replies: y
evals.expected_output: Skill stages only the explicit profile-redirect paths (no `git add -A`), runs the suspicious-file blocker (clean), runs `npm test` (pass), drafts a Conventional Commit-style subject like `fix: fix profile redirect` (≤70 chars) with Summary/Why/Behavior/Test Plan body sections and a `Co-Authored-By:` trailer naming the current Claude model identity, shows the bundled approval block (message + staged payload + no devjournal since this is a bugfix), awaits Y/n, then commits and pushes. Reports SHA + tracking info.
expectations: Only the explicit profile-redirect paths under src/ are staged — the command log contains no `git add -A` and no `git add .`
The suspicious-file blocker check reports clean (no refusal)
`npm test` runs and passes before any commit is made
The commit subject follows Conventional Commit style (`<type>: <summary>`), ≤70 chars
The commit body contains `Summary:`, `Why:`, `Behavior:`, and `Test Plan:` sections in that order

The commit trailers include a `Co-Authored-By:` line matching the pattern `Co-Authored-By: <any current Claude model identity> <noreply@anthropic.com>` — a pattern assertion, not a pinned model version (manifest note 5)
The bundled approval block presents the message, the staged payload, and omits a devjournal draft (bugfix, no trigger fires), and the skill waits for Y/n before committing
After approval, exactly one new commit exists in the sandbox git log, and `git push -u origin <branch>` appears in the command log
The final report includes the commit SHA and remote-tracking info

evals.preconditions: On a feature branch with a few edits to src/server/api.ts AND a modified `.env.local` in the working tree; no staging done yet.
evals.human_script: When the skill names `.env.local` as a suspicious-file match and asks for direction, the human replies: remove it from the payload.
evals.expected_output: Skill identifies `.env.local` as a suspicious-file blocker match (`.env*` rule), refuses to stage it, surfaces the filename, and asks the human for direction (remove from payload, include intentionally, or split). Does NOT proceed to `npm test` or commit. Does NOT use `git add -A`.
expectations: The response names `.env.local` as a suspicious-file blocker match before any staging occurs
No `git add` command in the command log stages `.env.local`
No new commit exists in the sandbox git log
`npm test` does not appear in the command log — the skill stops before the test gate
The command log contains no `git add -A` and no `git add .`

evals.preconditions: Issue 142 is open and describes a schema change. Payload contains only an expand change to `src/server/schema.ts` and a new `drizzle/0019_add_column.sql`. (Split from the original commit-3's sub-scenario (a) — manifest note.)
evals.human_script: At the step-14 bundled approval checkpoint, the human replies: y
evals.expected_output: Expand-only: schema-expand wait narration appears in the approval block; commit proceeds normally. Skill correctly identifies the expand phase, surfaces in the approval block that `/ship` will need to run `npm run prod:db:expand` after PR creation (with the `prod-db` maintainer-approval gate) and that the post-deploy `e2e.yml` must pass before merge, then proceeds normally through `npm test` + approval + commit + push.
expectations: The approval block narrates that `/ship` will need to run `npm run prod:db:expand` after PR creation, gated by the `prod-db` maintainer-approval environment
The approval block notes the post-deploy `e2e.yml` run must pass before `/ship` merges
The skill proceeds through `npm test`, the approval checkpoint, and commits and pushes normally — exactly one new commit exists in the sandbox git log
No refusal occurs anywhere in the transcript

evals.preconditions: Issue 142 is open and describes a schema change. Payload contains an expand change AND a contract change to `src/server/schema.ts` in the same commit (a column added and a different column dropped). (Split from the original commit-3's sub-scenario (b) — manifest note.)
evals.human_script: None — the skill refuses before any interactive checkpoint is reached; no scripted reply is required for this eval to complete.
evals.expected_output: Combined: hard refusal naming both phases; no commit happens. Skill refuses with `Refuse combined expand+contract payloads`, asks the human to split into two commits in deploy order (expand first, contract second).
expectations: The response contains an explicit refusal naming both the expand and contract phases (e.g. "Refuse combined expand+contract payloads")
The response asks the human to split into two commits in deploy order (expand first, contract second)
No new commit exists in the sandbox git log
`npm test` does not appear in the command log

evals.preconditions: Dirty feature branch (not main) with a few related edits; no .env or generated artifacts; gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local` opt-out file; not a delegated call (no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker); invoked by natural language (model/Skill-tool invocation, not a typed `/commit`).
evals.expected_output: The /commit Skill is invoked (the model routes the NL intent through the Skill rather than running an ad-hoc git sequence). Its first visible line is the announcement `Using /commit`. Its first ACTION (after that line) is the Step 0 intent gate via AskUserQuestion ("Run /commit with: <detected context>?") — no `gh auth`, no `git` commands run before it. Choosing Stop ends with zero side effects (no branch created, nothing staged, `git status` unchanged). Choosing Proceed continues into step 1 onward.
expectations: The first visible line of the response is the announcement `Using /commit`
The Step 0 intent gate fires via AskUserQuestion before any `gh auth` or `git` command runs
Choosing Stop leaves zero side effects: no branch created, nothing staged, `git status` unchanged
Choosing Proceed continues into step 1 onward
evals.notes: Routing eval — not sandbox-executable by a subagent (routing is the thing under test). Stays a documented manual runbook at baseline; automated by the Phase-8 session runner.

evals.preconditions:
Dirty feature branch; gh authenticated. Three sub-scenarios:
  (a) Explicit slash: the human types `/commit` (a `<command-name>` tag is present in the turn).
  (b) Delegation: `/pr` (or `/ship`->`/pr`) delegated here and created `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` immediately before the call.
  (c) Opt-out: `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local` exists; invoked by natural language.
evals.expected_output: In all three sub-scenarios the Step 0 intent confirmation does NOT fire and the Skill begins at step 1. In (b) the Skill deletes `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` on read (clear-on-read) so it cannot leak into a later standalone NL invocation. In (c) the opt-out file is left untouched. Announcement behavior differs by sub-scenario: in (c) opt-out the first visible line is still `Using /commit` (the opt-out file suppresses only the Step 0 confirmation, never the announcement); in (b) delegation the child does NOT self-announce — the delegation marker suppresses BOTH the confirmation AND the announcement, because the parent (`/pr` or `/ship`) already printed `Using /commit — delegated from /pr` on its behalf; in (a) the typed-slash path, no announcement is required. Control: a natural-language invocation with none of these conditions present DOES fire Step 0 (covered by commit-4).
expectations: (a) slash entry: Step 0 does not fire; the Skill begins at step 1; no announcement is required

(b) delegation: Step 0 does not fire; the Skill does NOT self-announce (parent already printed `Using /commit — delegated from /pr`); `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` is deleted on read (clear-on-read)
(c) opt-out: Step 0 does not fire; the first visible line is still `Using /commit`; the opt-out file itself is left untouched on disk
Control (commit-4): a natural-language invocation with none of these three conditions present DOES fire Step 0
evals.notes: Routing eval, no split (manifest resolves the flagged contradiction: the splitting rule applies to execution evals only). All three sub-scenarios stay one routing eval whose manual runbook — and later the Phase-8 runner — enumerates them as three queries. Named entry in the strategy doc's manual-runbook section (manifest cross-cutting requirement 4). AUTOMATION DIVERGENCE (#527/#528): the expectations above are the MANUAL-runbook checklist and remain correct for a real interactive session. The Phase-8 routing runner grades narrower versions via `expectationsOverride` in scripts/skill-evals/routing/routing-plan.mjs, because two things here cannot be observed headless: (b)'s clear-on-read — deleting inside `.claude/` is refused by built-in Claude Code protection, so marker deletion is checked ONLY by hand — and (a)'s no-Step-0 rule, which the runner relaxes because it cannot inject a kernel-level `<command-name>` tag, the condition SKILL.md Step 0 tells the skill to treat as ambiguous. Check both by hand; do not assume automation covers them. See docs/strategy-skill-evals.md §6.

evals.prompt: (assistant turn) "Want me to commit this?" then (human turn) "yes, go ahead"
evals.preconditions: Mid-conversation: the assistant has just finished a change and offered to commit it; the human replies only with a bare affirmation ("yes" / "go ahead" / "do it") and does NOT repeat the word "commit". Dirty feature branch (not main); gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local`; no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker.
evals.expected_output: The bare affirmation is treated as the commit trigger: the model invokes the /commit Skill via the Skill tool and does NOT hand-roll the commit with an ad-hoc `git add`/`git commit`/`git push` sequence. The first visible line is the announcement `Using /commit`, followed by the Step 0 intent gate. This guards the reported failure mode — the model reading a bare "yes" to its own offer as approval of an ad-hoc plan and committing directly, with no visible sign the Skill ran.
expectations: The bare affirmation routes through the Skill tool — no ad-hoc `git add`/`git commit`/`git push` sequence appears anywhere in the transcript
The first visible line is the announcement `Using /commit`
The Step 0 intent gate fires immediately after the announcement
evals.notes: Routing eval — two-turn; the Phase-8 multi-turn driver case (R4). Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline.

evals.prompt: (assistant turn) "Want me to explain how /commit's Step 0 works?" then (human turn) "yes"
evals.preconditions: Mid-conversation: the assistant's most recent offer was to EXPLAIN something about /commit (its Step 0, its mechanics) — an offer that names `/commit` but is NOT an offer to run a commit — and the human replies only with a bare affirmation ("yes" / "go ahead"). Dirty feature branch (not main); gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local`; no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker.
evals.expected_output: The bare "yes" is scoped to the offer that was actually made (to EXPLAIN /commit), so /commit does NOT fire: no `Using /commit` announcement, no Skill(commit) invocation, no Step 0, no staging or commit. The model explains /commit's Step 0 instead. Stronger negative control than an unrelated-topic offer (F2 / Thread 16): the offer literally contains the token `/commit`, so this proves the affirmation-is-the-trigger rule keys on the *intent* of the offer (to perform a commit), and is not fooled by the keyword appearing in a non-commit (explain) offer.
expectations: No `Using /commit` announcement appears anywhere in the response
The Skill tool is never invoked for /commit
No staging or commit command appears in the transcript
The model explains /commit's Step 0 instead of running it
evals.notes: Routing eval — negative control. Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline.

evals.prompt: does the commit message follow Conventional Commits?
evals.preconditions: The human asks a question ABOUT a commit message (its format / Conventional Commits compliance), not a request to perform a commit — e.g. reviewing an existing or drafted message. Tree may be clean or dirty; gh authenticated; no `.claude/skip-nl-confirm-commit-pr.local`; no `.claude/.nl-delegation-active` marker; natural language (not a typed `/commit`).
evals.expected_output: The model ANSWERS the question (assesses whether the message follows Conventional Commits) and does NOT fire /commit: no `Using /commit` announcement, no Skill(commit) invocation, no Step 0, no staging or commit. Over-trigger control (F3 / Thread 16): the word "commit" appearing as the *topic* of a question is not commit intent; only a request or affirmation to *perform* a commit routes through the Skill.
expectations: The model answers the Conventional Commits question directly
No `Using /commit` announcement appears anywhere in the response
The Skill tool is never invoked for /commit
No staging or commit command appears in the transcript
evals.notes: Routing eval — over-trigger control. Not sandbox-executable by a subagent at baseline.
